q: In 1678, the Dutch physicist Christiaan Huygens introduced the ___ theory of light.
Huygens' principle states that every point on a wavefront may be considered as a source of ___ wavelets.
The new position of wavefront is the ___ of the wavelets.
Secondary wavelets spread out in all directions with the speed ___.
A ___ is defined as a surface associated with a propagating wave which is a locus of all points in the wave that have the same phase.
At a given moment of time, all particles of the medium on a particular wavefront are undergoing the same ___.
Wavefront is always ___ to the direction of propagation (or) rays.
A ___ wavefront from a source transforms into plane wavefront when it is far away from the source.
The angular position of a point of a periodic wave is known as the ___.
The phase angle is the position of a point on a ___ circle of a wavefront at an instant.
If phase difference are $0, 2\pi, 4\pi, 6\pi, \dots$, these two positions are in ___.
If phase difference are $\pi, 3\pi, 5\pi, \dots$, these two positions are completely ___.
___ indicates the distance between two points measured along the direction of propagation of the wave through the medium.
If path difference is $0, \lambda, 2\lambda, 3\lambda, \dots$, the wave positions are said to be in ___.
If path difference are $\frac{\lambda}{2}, \frac{3\lambda}{2}, \frac{5\lambda}{2}, \dots$, it is said completely ___.
The relation between phase difference and path difference is expressed as $\Delta \phi = \frac{2\pi}{\lambda} ___$.
Wave optics includes the wave ___ of light.
Wave optics is also known as ___ optics.
Huygens' principle explained how light ___ from a light source.
Young was able to explain the alternate bright and dark lines as interference ___.
When a plane wave passes through a slit, a circular ___ emerges from it.
In the double slit experiment, each slit acts as a ___ source of circular waves.
Two waves are ___ if they are of same frequency, same amplitude and are in phase.
The interference pattern consists of a series of bright and dark parallel lines called ___.
___ interference occurs at the centre of the screen because waves travel the same distance.
The bright fringe at the centre of the screen is called ___.
If the path difference is equal to an ___ of wavelengths, the waves will interfere constructively.
If the path difference is equal to a ___ number of wavelengths, the waves will interfere destructively.
Constructive interference results in ___ fringes.
Destructive interference results in a ___ fringe on the screen.
The position of fringes is denoted by ___ which is the distance from the central bright fringe.
In the formula $d \sin \theta = m \lambda$, $m$ is called the ___ of bright fringe.
The zeroth order ($m = 0$) bright corresponds to the central bright line at $\theta = $ ___.
Distance between two adjacent bright (or dark) fringes is called the ___.
The position of fringes and fringe width depend on the ___ of light source.
Bright and dark fringes are formed alternatively where bright fringe has ___ intensity.
The intensity of a dark fringe in interference is ___.
___ light means that it has a single wavelength.
The distance of $m^{th}$ order bright fringe is given by $y_m = m \frac{\lambda D}{d}$ where $D$ is distance from ___ to screen.
___ is defined as the bending of waves around the corners of an obstacle.
The amount of bending depends on the ___ of light relative to the size of the aperture.
If the aperture is much ___ than the wavelength of light, the bending will be almost unnoticeable.
Light waves diffract around objects and create bright and dark ___.
In ___ diffraction, the source of light and the screen are at a finite distance from the obstacle.
In ___ diffraction, there is an infinite distance between the source and the screen from the obstacle.
The diffraction pattern of a single slit consists of a bright central portion called the ___.
Diffraction fringes are created by ___ of diffracted light waves.
Each successive bright fringe becomes ___ intense proceeding outward from the central maximum.
To produce a central bright maximum, secondary wavelets reach point $P_0$ in the same ___.
For the first minimum in a single slit, the path difference $a \sin \theta$ is equal to ___.
The position of $m^{th}$ minimum $y$ is the distance from the ___.
The width of the central maximum is equal to $2 \times$ position of the ___.
The width of the central maximum depends on the ___ of the slit.
A ___ slit causes a wider central maximum in diffraction.
A ___ consists of a large number of parallel slits of same width separated by equal opaque spaces.
A diffraction grating can ___ light into its wavelength components.
Diffraction grating is widely used as ___ elements in spectrographic instruments.
Two types of grating are ___ grating and reflection grating.
The number of lines (or slits) per unit length is called the ___.
The condition for maximum intensity in a diffraction grating is the same as that for a ___ double slit.
For maximum intensity, the path difference $d \sin \theta$ must equal ___.
In a grating, $d$ is the distance between ___ slits.
If the number of slits per unit length is $N$, then $d = $ ___.
There is no ___ of incident white light at the centre ($m=0$) of a grating spectrum.
In the first order diffraction spectrum, ___ light diffracts at a greater angle than violet light.
A ___ is an example of reflection gratings.
A reflection grating is a shiny surface having many fine ___.
The tracks of a compact disc act as a diffraction grating, producing a separation of the colors of ___ light.
Diffraction grating is a crucial tool in the study of ___ and molecular compositions.
